episodes.title: Is Jehovah’s Witness a Cult? Disfellowshipping, Blue Envelopes, and Trauma
episodes.description:
What happens when the religion you were raised in starts to feel a lot more like a cult?

In this raw, unfiltered episode of Pissy But Pretty, Emily goes deep into her experience growing up as a Jehovah’s Witness, being disfellowshipped, facing a panel of elders, and navigating life after shunning. From the “two witness rule” to the Blue Envelope reports and cult rehabilitation, this conversation pulls zero punches. If you have ever questioned high control religion, religious trauma, or what it actually takes to rebuild your identity after being excommunicated, buckle up.

This is not polite church talk. This is what happens when two middle aged women who used to party hard start unpacking spiritual control, codependency, and survival with brutal honesty.

#JehovahsWitness #ReligiousTrauma #ExJW #CultRecovery #SpiritualAbuse #PissyButPretty

What You’ll Learn:

How disfellowshipping and shunning operate inside Jehovah’s Witness culture
Why the “two witness rule” created systemic protection for abusers
What Blue Envelope incident reports are and how they were handled
The psychological impact of religious trauma and spiritual fear conditioning
Why cult rehabilitation and reintegration therapy matter after leaving a high control group
Why so many ex members go back and what it takes to stay out

Timestamps / Chapters:

00:00 – Who are we and why are we talking about cults on Palm Sunday?
06:29 – What makes a religion a cult and how control actually works
14:09 – Disfellowshipping vs shunning and why language softens abuse
17:05 – What are Blue Envelope reports and how were assault claims handled?
19:15 – The two witness rule and why victims were rarely believed
21:07 – What happens inside a judicial committee with three elders?
28:35 – What is cult rehabilitation and how do you reintegrate into real life?
37:25 – Why do so many disfellowshipped members fight to get back in?
49:35 – How life gets exponentially better after leaving a high control religion
